sections.paragraph:
m. plur. constr. יַלְדֵי and יִלְדֵי ( Isa 57:4)-

(1) one born, a son, poetically i.q. בֵּן. Hence יַלְדֵי נָכְרִים sons of strangers, poet. for strangers (compare בֵּן p. 125, B .), sons of wickedness, for the Wicked, Isa 57:4. Used κατʼ ἐξοχὴν, of the king’s son [rather, the son of God, himself the mighty God], Isa 9:5. Plur. comm. children, i.q. בָּנִים 1Sa 1:2 Ezr 10:1 also of the young of animals, Isa 11:7 Job 38:41.

(2) a child, sometimes one recently born, Gen 21:8, seq.; Exo 1:17 2:3 , seq.; sometimes one older, a young man, Gen 4:23 (in the other hemistich אִישׁ). Ecc 4:13 1Ki 12:8 (opp. to זָקֵן).
